title: Health endpoint (server-probe verified)
goal: Implement a stdlib HTTP server with /health, verified by hitting it.
prompt: Create `app.py` using ONLY the Python standard library (no third-party packages such as Flask). Running `python3 app.py` must start an HTTP server on the port given by the PORT environment variable (default 8765 if unset) that responds to GET /health with HTTP status 200 and body exactly `ok`. The server should keep running until terminated.

verify.cmd:
python3 - <<'PY'
import os, subprocess, sys, time, urllib.request, random
port = random.randint(49152, 65535)
p = subprocess.Popen([sys.executable, 'app.py'], env={**os.environ, 'PORT': str(port)})
try:
    for _ in range(20):
        time.sleep(0.5)
        try:
            r = urllib.request.urlopen(f'http://localhost:{port}/health', timeout=1)
            if r.status == 200 and r.read().strip() == b'ok':
                sys.exit(0)
        except Exception:
            pass
    sys.exit(1)
finally:
    p.terminate()
PY
